title: nasturtium coronopifolium var. pumilum
intro: Nasturtium coronopifolium var. pumilum is a rare, low-growing variety of nasturtium, a flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, known for its delicate white or pale yellow flowers and small, rounded leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Nasturtium coronopifolium var. pumilum has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of nasturtium coronopifolium var. pumilum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
